channel.title: CTV News – Queen's Park
channel.description: Queen's Park coverage from CTV News Toronto.

item.title: ‘Reckless and disrespectful’: Pennsylvania governor slams Trump’s Canada comments as he signs trade pact with Ontario
‘Everyone’s going to be kumbaya’: Ford reiterates U.S. booze will go back on store shelves once trade deal is signed
item.description: Ontario Premier Doug Ford said again Tuesday that he won’t put U.S. alcohol back on LCBO shelves in Ontario until a trade deal is hammered out with the U.S. to end the ongoing trade war.

item.title: ‘They treat us so well’: Doug Ford responds after reception with American billionaire is cancelled
item.description: A planned reception with Doug Ford and American billionaire Ross Perot Jr. in D.C. was cancelled Monday, according to the U.S. Chamber of Commerce.

item.title: Housing advocate Eric Lombardi joins Ontario Liberal leadership race
item.description: Housing advocate Eric Lombardi is officially joining the race to lead Ontario’s Liberal party and “bring back an affordable life.”

item.title: Federal government launches public consultations on Billy Bishop airport
item.description: The federal government says it is launching consultations on the future of Toronto’s Billy Bishop airport as the Ford government moves to expand the airport into a busier facility that could accommodate higher passenger volumes, as well as jets.

item.title: Most OSAP growth came from career college students, documents show
item.description: When Ontario Premier Doug Ford’s government drastically cut student assistance grants earlier this year, it cited “unsustainable” costs, but new figures show nearly all of the recent growth was among career college students.

item.title: Unionized workers in Ontario rally for lost wages, transparency on $1B spent on privately administered government programs
item.description: Hundreds of striking or locked-out public sector workers marched through Toronto’s streets earlier this week, as part of a coordinated series of rallies designed to show solidarity to some 4,000 workers provincewide.

item.title: Report says Billy Bishop airport expansion could impact thousands of homes along waterfront
item.description: A new report from city staff is warning that any expansion of Billy Bishop airport could result in major adverse impacts on housing, congestion, the environment and the waterfront as a whole.

item.title: Carney has not formed opinion on Ford's island airport expansion vision in Toronto
item.description: Prime Minister Mark Carney says he has not formed an opinion on Ontario Premier Doug Ford’s plan to expand Toronto’s island airport.

item.title: ‘We got to stop treating Ontario like a banana republic’: Ford defends use of charter flights
item.description: Just weeks after selling a private jet, Ontario Premier Doug Ford is now defending his use of chartering flights, suggesting “that’s the best investment” for his frequent travel across the province and the United Sates.

item.title: Ontario will name and shame businesses fined for selling tickets above face value
item.description: The Ontario government is more than doubling the maximum fine that can be handed out for selling tickets above their original value ahead of the upcoming FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Ontario civil servants to see ‘flexibility’ on work options during World Cup, government says
item.description: Ontario’s incoming interim Treasury Board president says there will be “flexibility” in work arrangements for public service employees during the upcoming FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Ontario legislature to rise for 21-week break following short session
item.description: Ontario’s legislature started a 21-week break on Tuesday and by the time members of provincial parliament return from the summer recess, it will nearly be Halloween.

item.title: Over $1 billion goes to private companies administering Ontario government programs: OPSEU report
item.description: A new report authored by a public sector union whose members are striking for a second week is calling attention to how much more public money is going to private companies that administer government services.

item.title: Ontario has engaged with FIFA over alleged non-compliance with its new ticket resale law
item.description: The Ontario government says FIFA may be offside of its new law banning the resale of tickets for above face value and is warning the international soccer organization it could face a fine of up to $250,000 for doing so.
